content.text:
Perfect! Here's the exact markup pattern used in the Station form: ## Station Form Markup Pattern (Lines 122-139) ```html

General information

@Html.LabelFor(y => y.Facility_Name, new { @class = "m-0 fs-14" }) @Html.DropDownListFor(y => y.Facility_Key, (IEnumerable)Model.FacilityList, new { @class = "md-input" })
@Html.LabelFor(y => y.StationName, new { @class = "m-0 fs-14" }) @Html.TextBoxFor(y => y.StationName, new { @class = "md-input", id = "txtmachinename" })
@Html.LabelFor(y => y.Alias, new { @class = "m-0 fs-14" }) @Html.TextBoxFor(y => y.Alias, new { @class = "md-input" })
``` ## Key Pattern Details **Container Structure:** - `uk-grid` for the outer container - `uk-width-1-3@s` for 3-column responsive layout (33% width on small+ screens) - `uk-width-1-1` for full-width section headers **Form Field Pattern:** - **Label classes:** `m-0 fs-14` (no margin, font-size 14px) - **Input classes:** `md-input` (Material Design input styling) - **No input-group wrappers** - direct label + input structure - **No explicit form-group divs** - the `uk-width-*` divs serve as field containers **Notable Features:** - Uses UIkit grid system (`uk-grid`, `uk-width-*`) - Responsive breakpoints with `@s` suffix - Material Design styling via `md-input` and `md-card` - Tab navigation with `uk-tab` and `uk-switcher` This is a simpler pattern than Bootstrap's input-group - just grid columns containing label + input pairs.
